preamble: The voiceless aspirated retroflex affricate (ʈʂʰ) can undergo a process of lenition, leading to its complete deletion or reduction to a zero sound in certain linguistic contexts. This phenomenon represents a significant weakening of the consonant, often observed in rapid or casual speech.
phoneticEffects: Lenition, deletion, consonant reduction, affricate loss

examples.note: In Beijing Mandarin, the retroflex obstruent onset, including /ʈʂʰ/, can be completely deleted in fast speech as a gradient outcome of retroflex lenition.

sources: Source via Google Search grounding (verify before merge)
